summary: Proposal-only red review after L77: reject the concise prompt latency candidate because it bought only a tiny latency change while breaking abstention, and run at most a tightly gated top_k latency diagnostic after the current guards, not a broad promotion sweep.

models.review_method: Read-only local artifact inspection and proposal writing; no evaluator, generator, retriever, judge, API, or model call was run.

outputs.description: Proposal-only contract for the safest next latency/cost experiment after L77.

proposal.bottom_line: Do not promote L77 and do not rerun prompt_concise_verified as a latency path. The next safest experiment is a small guarded top_k latency diagnostic using the L76 baseline prompt and current guards, with top_k treated as a candidate for reducing context/generation cost only if it preserves L76's exposed-regression correctness.

l77_failure_diagnosis.cause: L77 changed the prompt to concise_verified while keeping top_k=8 and the current retrieval guards. The change did not materially reduce latency or cost and weakened abstention behavior on the exposed v6 regression set.
l77_failure_diagnosis.insight: L77 is a quality regression disguised as a tiny speed change. The failure is not retrieval coverage, groundedness, or relevance; those stayed saturated. The decisive failure is abstention accuracy collapse, specifically the ambiguous-fragment negative-control path answering instead of refusing. Generation remains the dominant latency/cost component, so prompt concision is not safe unless it preserves abstention first.

top_k_sweep_after_current_guards.recommended_scope: Run a narrow top_k micro-sweep after the current guards only if the orchestrator wants latency/cost evidence and accepts that exposed v6 is regression stability evidence, not fresh validation.
not_worth_running_as: a promotion run on exposed v6 alone
a repeat of L39 without current guards
a replacement for fresh holdout validation
why_conditional: Retrieval cost is near zero, but lower top_k may reduce context tokens, answer-generation time, judge prompt size, and total observed cost.
Prior L39 showed top_k alone did not fix qv6_007 and top12 lowered groundedness; therefore top_k is not a correctness strategy.
Prior top5 regression evidence from other loops warned that top_k can expose quality regressions despite apparent speed hopes.
After L76, the current guards changed the risk profile enough that a tiny top_k latency diagnostic is reasonable, but only with strict rejection gates.

next_safest_experiment.name: guarded_topk_latency_micro_sweep_after_l77
guards_required_before_interpretation: answer cleanup guards used by L76
next_safest_experiment.run_shape: one small judged sweep only if budget preflight estimates the whole run below 0.25 USD; otherwise start with no-judge or saved-answer diagnostics and stop before paid expansion
next_safest_experiment.expected_value: Tests whether reducing retrieved context lowers generation/judge tokens without reopening the L77 abstention regression.

acceptance_gates.accept_if: candidate EDD >= 97.13 or no worse than L76 on all raw safety-critical metrics, with abstention_accuracy exactly 1.0, groundedness_avg 5.0, relevance_avg 5.0, retrieval_coverage_avg 1.0, hit_all_targets_rate 1.0, false_abstention_rate 0.0, and empty_answer_rate 0.0
acceptance_gates.reject_if: any abstention-sensitive case answers when it should abstain, any false abstention appears, any empty answer appears, or groundedness/relevance drops below L76

acceptance_gates.accept_if: latency_avg_sec improves by at least 10 percent versus L76, or improves by at least 2.0 seconds absolute while all quality gates pass
acceptance_gates.reject_if: latency improvement is below 1.0 second, latency_avg_sec is worse than L76, or two or more individual shared cases regress by at least 5.0 seconds

acceptance_gates.accept_if: observed_cost_usd is below L76 by at least 5 percent, or token/cost decomposition shows answer_generation plus judge cost fell without quality loss
acceptance_gates.reject_if: observed_cost_usd exceeds L76, cost ledger has missing usage, unknown price rows appear, or preflight projected spend exceeds the proposed 0.25 USD micro-sweep ceiling

acceptance_gates.accept_if: qv6_007 claim/evidence-use gate remains 2/2 and all L76 guard-sensitive cases keep their expected labels
acceptance_gates.reject_if: any guard that was clean in L76 becomes non-clean, especially qv6_010 ambiguous abstention or qv6_007 physical access-control inclusion

acceptance_gates.accept_if: result is labeled diagnostic_only unless repeated on a fresh holdout or first-validation pack
acceptance_gates.reject_if: exposed v6 top_k success is presented as general validation, a champion, or a broad correctness improvement

rejection_gates: Reject any candidate repeating L77's abstention_accuracy=0.5 pattern regardless of latency.
Reject any candidate whose EDD improvement is driven only by latency while safety or evidence-use metrics regress.
Reject top_k lower than 8 if it drops MRR, hit-all-targets, or target-only retrieval on any guard-sensitive case.
Reject any result with cost_summary missing, budget_ledger missing, usage_missing=true, or unknown_price_model=true.
Reject any run that modifies protected paths or updates scoreboards/logs from a proposal-only worker.

overfitting: The v6 cohort is exposed and has been repeatedly tuned against through L76, so a top_k pass on it is regression stability evidence only.
qv6_007 and qv6_010 have named guards; optimizing top_k around them can hide unrelated fresh-case failures.
Near-ceiling EDD on exposed rows can make small latency wins look safer than they are.
exposed_regression: A lower top_k may keep exposed target coverage while losing supporting evidence diversity needed for fresh or paraphrased cases.
The current guards may catch known failures but not new wrong-document, source-scope, or underanswering patterns.
Judge scores were previously blind to qv6_007-style incompleteness, so guard reports must override judge 5/5 when they disagree.
latency_cost_interpretation: Retrieval is cheap; most latency and cost are generation/judge, so top_k only matters if it materially reduces context and completion tokens.
Single-run latency variance can exceed the 0.53s L77 apparent gain, so acceptance needs a material threshold.
A no-judge diagnostic can reduce spend but cannot promote a candidate without later judged verification.

recommended_cause_result_insight_record.cause: L77 tried prompt concision as a latency lever after L76 passed exposed regression guards.
recommended_cause_result_insight_record.result: It reduced average latency by only 0.53s, increased observed cost by 0.00432 USD, and dropped abstention accuracy from 1.0 to 0.5, lowering EDD from 97.13 to 92.25.
recommended_cause_result_insight_record.insight: The next latency experiment should change retrieval/context size, not refusal wording, and must make abstention and claim gates hard blockers before any speed/cost win is considered.

observed_results: No paid API/model calls were made.
No evaluator, generator, retriever, or judge flow was run.
The target output file did not exist before this proposal was written.
L76 metrics: EDD 97.13, latency_avg_sec 20.642, abstention_accuracy 1.0, observed_cost_usd 0.141636.
L77 metrics: EDD 92.25, latency_avg_sec 20.112, abstention_accuracy 0.5, observed_cost_usd 0.145956.
L77 cost ledger had calls_recorded 28 and no missing cost rows, so the failure is not cost telemetry.
L39 prior top_k probe was diagnostic only and top_k alone did not fix the qv6_007 failure.
Existing proposal contracts use schema=parallel_team_worker_output.v1 and nested proposal fields; this contract follows that pattern.
